prompt:
The packing-line label printer has been misbehaving ever since I ported its dispatch-label renderer from the old desktop app, and the warehouse team is annoyed. Three separate complaints, all on every label it prints:

- The label number tracks OUR packing-app session counter (restarts at 1 whenever the app restarts). It is supposed to show the printer's own running number — the firmware no longer receives its substitution token from us.
- The printer-help line comes out mangled: the file path gets chopped across two lines with a weird indent in the middle, like the label suddenly grew extra lines.
- There are stray backslashes printed around the quoted handling note in the footer.

The label text is right otherwise — recipient, route, and depot are all fine, and the status-bar counter in the app still has to count rendered labels like it does today.

Contract is test_dispatchlabel.py: `python3 test_dispatchlabel.py` must pass. Fix the renderer, hands off the test file.
notes: Replacement identity for the safeguard-rejected py-visitpass fixture; exact escaping mechanics and catalog slot preserved. Both rejected Fable attempts are retained under traces/archive and must remain Git-only.
